data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260513T182542-83fcd146/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260513T182542-83fcd146/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260513T182542-83fcd146/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260513T182542-83fcd146/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260513T182542-83fcd146/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-c81c3cad">
    <title>readiness: normalize malformed readiness_checks payloads</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
During plain `ddx work` runs on 2026-05-13, the pre-claim readiness hook repeatedly printed raw schema errors such as `readiness check unavailable: decode result: json: cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field .readiness_checks of type []struct { Reason string ... } (continuing)` and, from a concurrent Codex worker, `cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field .readiness_checks ...`. DDx continued, but the operator-visible message is a raw Go JSON decode error and the malformed readiness output is treated as hook-unavailable instead of being normalized into actionable evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:56-67` defines readiness classification payloads with `readiness_checks []preClaimReadinessCheck`. `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:307-323` probes `readiness_checks` as an array before deciding which decoder to use, so model/hook responses with `readiness_checks` as an object or string fail the entire probe decode. Existing tests cover empty output and valid array payloads, but not malformed singleton object or scalar-shaped `readiness_checks`.

PROPOSED FIX
Make readiness decoding resilient to `readiness_checks` being a singleton object or scalar string. Normalize a singleton object to a one-element slice. For scalar/string values, return a structured malformed-readiness result with a concise actionable message naming `readiness_checks`, preserving the value as evidence if useful, and preserving forward progress. The worker log should not expose raw Go struct types.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the readiness classification vocabulary. Do not make malformed readiness output a bead blocker unless the configured quality mode already blocks system-unready hook failures. Do not change implementation dispatch routing.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestPreClaimReadiness_NormalizesSingletonReadinessChecksObject` exists and proves a payload with `"readiness_checks":{"reason":"missing_verification","verdict":"fail","evidence":"AC lacks go test"}` is handled deterministically instead of failing the probe unmarshal.
2. `TestPreClaimReadiness_NormalizesScalarReadinessChecksString` exists and proves a payload with `"readiness_checks":"missing_verification"` produces a structured malformed-readiness result or normalized check instead of failing the probe unmarshal.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerStdout_ReadinessMalformedSchemaIsActionable` exists and proves worker output contains a concise message naming `readiness_checks` without printing a raw Go struct type for either object- or string-shaped malformed payloads.
4. Existing readiness tests including `TestReadinessHookEmptyOutput_DeduplicatesPrefix`, `TestReadinessUnavailableOutputIsActionable`, and `TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesCanonicalReadinessJSON` remain green.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreClaimReadiness_.*|TestExecuteBeadWorkerStdout_Readiness.*|TestReadiness.*" -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:agent, area:bead-lifecycle, kind:bug, phase:build, reliability</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="b8306054f33cdd02d82ea4e22ce1c7933d5e9da8" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260513T182542-83fcd146"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented in `dde80c71b` with subject `fix: normalize malformed readiness_checks payloads [ddx-c81c3cad]`.

What changed:
- Added a custom `readiness_checks` JSON decoder that:
  - normalizes a singleton object into a one-element slice
  - treats scalar/string values as a structured malformed-readiness result with an actionable `readiness_checks` message
- Updated the readiness classification probe so object/string shapes no longer fail the initial unmarshal.
- Added regression coverage for:
  - singleton-object payloads
  - scalar/string payloads
  - worker stdout formatting without raw Go struct-type errors

Key refs:
- Decoder and normalization logic: [cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c81c3cad-20260513T182542-83fcd146/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go#L98-L139)
- Probe and malformed handling: [cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c81c3cad-20260513T182542-83fcd146/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go#L351-L503)
- Object/string regression tests: [cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c81c3cad-20260513T182542-83fcd146/cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go#L150-L168)
- Worker stdout regression test: [cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c81c3cad-20260513T182542-83fcd146/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go#L719-L785)

Verified:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestPreClaimReadiness_.*|TestExecuteBeadWorkerStdout_Readiness.*|TestReadiness.*' -count=1`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`
